stem: A agriculture study measures crop yield across 3 regions, each containing multiple farms. region A has 10 observations with mean 38.0 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 6.1). region B has 5 observations with mean 56.4 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 5.9). region C has 5 observations with mean 48.7 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 4.7). The between-region standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 4.6 and 11.6 tonnes/hectare. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true crop yield for region C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-region variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for region C: 48.5 tonnes/hectare (shrunk from raw 48.7). Range [48.1, 48.6] as between-region SD varies from 4.6 to 11.6. Pooled mean: 45.3.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 45.3 tonnes/hectare (weighted average of 3 region means).
Shrinkage factor for region C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=7.7: B = 0.931.
Interval [48.1, 48.6] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (48.7) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (45.3) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
